notes: Explicit, unambiguous no-sponsor statement plus a permanent FT role.
text: We are hiring a Software Engineer to join our growing product team. This is a full-time, permanent position based in Toronto. Please note: we do not sponsor employment visas or work permits for this role, now or in the future. All applicants must already hold valid Canadian work authorization.

notes: Government-adjacent role restricted to citizens/PR — classic hard no-sponsor case.
text: Due to the nature of this federal contract, applicants must be a Canadian citizen or permanent resident to be considered. Security clearance required. This role is not eligible for any form of sponsorship.

notes: Direct, explicit statement that sponsorship is offered.
text: We're excited to grow our engineering team! Sponsorship is available for the right candidate, and we have a track record of supporting employees through the work permit process. Apply today.

notes: Co-op posting with explicit LMIA support language and welcoming to study permit holders.
text: Summer Co-op Student, Data Analytics. This is a 4-month co-op placement as part of your academic program. LMIA support is available for eligible candidates, and study permit holders are encouraged to apply.

notes: On-campus student job, zero sponsorship language either way -- should be unclear on sponsorship but tagged on_campus_only.
text: The University Library is hiring a part-time on-campus Circulation Assistant. 12-15 hours per week, flexible scheduling around your class timetable. Must be a currently enrolled student.

notes: Weak/ambiguous authorization phrasing without explicit 'without sponsorship' -- genuinely ambiguous case, could go either way, we label it unclear to test whether the heuristic over-triggers on the weak pattern.
text: Candidates should have current work authorization for Canada. We are a small consulting firm looking for a detail-oriented analyst to join our downtown office.

notes: Retail seasonal job, no sponsorship or eligibility signal at all.
text: Seasonal Sales Associate needed for the holiday rush. Weekend and evening availability required. Retail experience an asset but not required. Apply in person.

notes: Startup explicitly welcoming international students -- positive signal without being a formal 'sponsorship' statement.
text: We're a 20-person startup building fintech tools for small businesses. International students are welcome to apply -- we've sponsored work permits for past hires and are happy to do it again for the right person.

notes: Co-op posting with zero sponsorship language -- category should still fire, sponsorship stays unclear.
text: Engineering Co-op (Winter Term). Work alongside our embedded systems team on real production hardware. This placement counts toward your program's work-integrated learning requirement.

notes: Bank new-grad program, full-time permanent, no sponsorship statement either way.
text: Our New Grad Program is a full-time, permanent entry point into our technology division. Rotational placements across three business units over 18 months.

notes: Explicitly accepts open work permit holders / any legal status -- unrestricted tag plus a positive sponsorship-adjacent signal.
text: We welcome applicants of any legal work status, including open work permit holders. If you're authorized to work in Canada in any capacity, we'd love to hear from you.

notes: Weak negative signal only (permanent work authorization required) with no explicit sponsorship statement -- should tip unlikely but with lower confidence than a hard no-sponsor case.
text: Applicants must have valid permanent work authorization for this role. We are unable to consider candidates requiring any future work permit renewals or extensions for this position.

notes: Deliberately contradictory posting (copy-pasted boilerplate error) with both a sponsor and no-sponsor phrase -- true edge case, should land unclear/mixed.
text: Sponsorship is available for exceptional candidates. Standard disclaimer: we do not sponsor employment visas for this position. Please disregard whichever line does not apply to your team's current hiring approval.

notes: On-campus research assistant that is also part of a co-op program -- both categories should fire together.
text: On-campus Research Assistant Co-op position in the Faculty of Science. This role satisfies your program's practicum requirement and is based entirely on university premises.

notes: Explicit, unambiguous statement that visa sponsorship is not offered, phrased differently from p01/p02 to test pattern generalization.
text: Please note that our company does not currently provide immigration sponsorship for any position, including this one. We encourage candidates who already have work authorization to apply.

notes: Explicit generic sponsorship-available statement in a career-track full-time role -- likely plus pgwp_track.
text: This is a full-time, permanent Business Analyst role. We are open to sponsoring qualified candidates and have supported multiple work permit applications in the past two years.

notes: Internship (not formally 'co-op') explicitly requiring citizenship -- unlikely, and co_op pattern should still fire on 'internship' even though sponsorship is unlikely, since eligibility category and sponsorship likelihood are independent axes.
text: Marketing Internship -- must be a Canadian citizen or permanent resident due to government funding requirements for this internship program.

notes: Plain job ad with zero signal in either direction and zero category language -- pure baseline unclear case.
text: We are looking for a friendly, organized office coordinator to manage day-to-day administrative tasks. Prior experience with scheduling software preferred.

notes: ADVERSARIAL: past-tense negation ('used to sponsor... no longer'). True intent is unlikely, but the v0 patterns only match present-tense 'we sponsor'/'we do not sponsor' phrasing and are not expected to catch this -- included deliberately to give an honest, non-inflated eval number and document a real gap.
text: We used to sponsor work permits for international hires, but as of this year we no longer offer that support due to budget constraints.

notes: ADVERSARIAL: negation phrased as 'not open to candidates who require sponsorship' -- semantically a clear no-sponsor statement but does not match any v0 regex (no 'we do not sponsor' / 'sponsorship not available' style phrasing). Included to honestly surface a coverage gap rather than hide it.
text: This role is not open to candidates who require sponsorship of any kind, now or in the future.
